prerequisites: A Klaviyo account with an Owner or Admin role, which is required to create a private API key.
A private API key created under Settings > API keys. For a read-only source sync, a Read-Only scope key is sufficient.
Optionally, a start_date to bound the initial backfill, and specific metric_ids if you only want to sync selected metrics.

authMethods.name: Private API key
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single Klaviyo private API key supplied in the required secret field api_key. Klaviyo sends this key on each request via the HTTP header 'Authorization: Klaviyo-API-Key <private-api-key>'. Private keys are prefixed with pk_ and can be scoped Read-Only, Full, or Custom; a Read-Only key is enough for extracting data.

setupSteps.title: Create a private API key in Klaviyo
setupSteps.body: In Klaviyo, click your organization name (bottom left), go to Settings, then API keys, and select Create Private API Key. Name the key and choose a scope; Read-Only is sufficient for a source connector. Click Create and copy the key immediately, because Klaviyo will not show it again after creation.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key
setupSteps.body: Paste the private API key into the required api_key field. The connector uses it to authenticate every request via the Klaviyo-API-Key authorization header.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window and scope (optional)
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to control how far back the initial backfill reaches, and lookback_window to re-pull recent records on each run. Use metric_ids to limit syncing to specific metrics. Set disable_fetching_predictive_analytics to skip predictive analytics fields, and num_workers to tune request concurrency. All of these are optional.

sources.title: How to create or clone a private API key | Klaviyo Help Center
